replication_study.hypothesis: Among participants who correctly identify the lowest possible outcome, mean willingness to pay (WTP) for a 50/50 $10/$20 prospect framed as a lottery ticket is lower than mean WTP for the same prospect framed as an uncertain gift certificate (WTP_lottery < WTP_gift).

data_plan.source_type: Online experimental survey (Qualtrics; MTurk-style design as per codebook notes and variable structure).
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Single replication focusing on lottery vs gift frame (buyer/WTP task), analogous to Experiment 1 in the original paper.
data_plan.sample_size: Approx. N=281 total rows in the file; analysis subset is those with non-missing WTP and correct comprehension (check==3). Final N determined at runtime by the script.
data_plan.access_details: Provided locally in data/original/32/input/replication_data. For execution, place or mount the CSV at /app/data/Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv.

qualification.explanation: This dataset contains an independent sample collected under a highly similar design to the original Experiment 1, enabling a direct replication of the focal WTP framing effect using a distinct participant pool and collection instance while maintaining fidelity to operational definitions.
qualification.similarity_to_original: • Both measure WTP (USD) for a 50/50 risky prospect framed as ‘lottery’ vs ‘gift card’. Original details: Experiment 1 used $10/$20 frames with attention and lowest-outcome checks (original_paper.pdf, pp. 726–728, Experiment 1 Method and Results). The replication data include Lot_WTPc, Gift_WTPc and comprehension checks with correct response coded as 3 (replication_data/Script.R codebook; Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv columns Lot_WTPc, Gift_WTPc, Lot_check, Gift_check). • Original recruited MTurk participants (post_registration.json: original_study.data.source; original_paper.pdf Experiment 1 Method). The replication codebook notes removal of Qualtrics metadata, consistent with an online survey platform (Script.R comments).
qualification.deviation_from_original: • Original Experiment 1 used incentive-compatible payoffs with a random bonus purchase opportunity (original_paper.pdf, p. 727). The replication dataset appears hypothetical (presence of very large free-text WTP entries such as 7000, 200000 in Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv) and no explicit incentive-compatible mechanism indicated (Script.R comments). • Sample sizes differ (original n=119; post_registration.json, Experiment 1), whereas replication CSV shows approximately 281 rows. • Replication focuses on WTP only (buyer task) for two frames (lottery vs gift), omitting WTA and other frame labels tested in later experiments (post_registration.json method; original_paper.pdf Experiments 2–4).

data_plan.notes: Data include currency-like strings (e.g., "$20", "$ 20"); the Python script cleans non-numeric characters. Some WTP values are extreme outliers; the script also provides a sensitivity subset (WTP<=20) analogous to the R script. The comprehension check is applied via check==3 as in the R code. The CSV first column appears as a stray index (e.g., 'ï..Id'); it is ignored. Ensure the CSV is available at /app/data/Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv.

steps: Construct Cond (lottery vs gift) using Lot_check and Gift_check presence, as in the original R logic.
Construct WTP from Lot_WTPc (if lottery) or Gift_WTPc (if gift).
Filter to participants who correctly identified the lowest possible outcome (check==3).
Compute group summaries (mean, sd, median) by Cond.
Run Welch independent-samples t-test for WTP between gift vs lottery (primary test).
Compute Hedges g (Cohen’s d with small-sample correction) for effect size (gift minus lottery).
Sensitivity: Repeat analysis with subset WTP<=20.
Export filtered dataset and result summaries to /app/data.
planned_method.models: Independent-samples comparison: Welch t-test on WTP (gift vs lottery). Nonparametric comparison can be added if needed, but core replication uses parametric test as per R code.
planned_method.outcome_variable: WTP (willingness to pay) in USD (numeric, cleaned).
planned_method.control_variables: None; analysis applies comprehension check filter (check==3) as a precondition rather than covariate.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Difference in means (WTP_gift - WTP_lottery); standardized effect size (Hedges g).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion for missing WTP or Cond; numeric coercion with non-numeric entries set to NaN and excluded; filter to check==3.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Not applicable (single primary outcome and contrast).
planned_method.inference_criteria: Two-tailed alpha=0.05; expected direction WTP_gift > WTP_lottery. Report effect size and p-value; optionally 95% CI for means/difference if computed.

files.replication_data/Script__py.py: Python translation of the provided R analysis (Script.R). Reads /app/data/Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv (or REPLICATION_INPUT env var), cleans WTP fields, constructs Cond and check, filters to check==3, runs Welch t-test (gift vs lottery) and Hedges g, runs sensitivity for WTP<=20, writes dataS.csv and results_summary.{json,txt} to /app/data. This is the execution entrypoint.

codebase.notes: All I/O is constrained to /app/data for compatibility. The original R script (replication_data/Script.R) is not executed and remains unmodified. The Python script preserves original analysis logic and adds robust numeric cleaning for currency-like inputs.

instructions: Ensure the dataset is available as /app/data/Data_Cleaned_22102020.csv (e.g., mount ./data to /app/data).
Outputs generated at /app/data: dataS.csv (filtered dataset), results_summary.json, results_summary.txt.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Compare direction (gift > lottery), statistical significance (p<0.05), and magnitude (Hedges g) to the original Experiment 1 (original_paper.pdf: WTP lottery M=$5.68 vs gift M=$9.52; t(99)=3.43, p<.001). Assess robustness via sensitivity subset (WTP<=20) and qualitative concordance with post_registration.json numerical summaries.
